Class UserService

java.lang.Object
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.service.security.UserService
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService
@Service public class UserService extends Object implements org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService

  • Constructor Details

    • UserService

      public UserService()

  • Method Details

    • getAllUsers

      public List<PUser> getAllUsers()

    • createUser

      public PUser createUser(PUser user)
      Creates a user. The username must not be taken yet — see requireUsernameFree(String, Long).
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the username is already in use (mapped to 409)

    • findOptionalByUsername

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Optional<PUser> findOptionalByUsername(String username)
      Looks a user up by name without hiding deactivated accounts, unlike findUserByUsername(String). Used where the caller has to see that a name is occupied rather than whether the account is usable.

    • loadUserByUsername

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails loadUserByUsername(String username) throws org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UsernameNotFoundException
      Specified by:
      loadUserByUsername in interface org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService
      Throws:
      org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UsernameNotFoundException

    • findUserByUsername

      public PUser findUserByUsername(String username)

    • updateUser

      @Transactional public PUser updateUser(PUser user)

    • getUserById

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public PUser getUserById(Long id)

    • partialUpdateUser

      @Transactional public PUser partialUpdateUser(Long id, PUser patch)

    • getAddress

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Address getAddress(Long id)
      Reads the user's address as a detached copy, initialized inside this transaction (see CompanyService.getMainAddress(Long, PUser)). Returns null if the user has no address.

    • deactivateUser

      @Transactional public void deactivateUser(Long id)

    • getRoleIds

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Set<Long> getRoleIds(Long userId)
      Returns the ids of the roles currently assigned to the user. Because PUser.roles is a lazy collection, this must be read inside a transaction — the caller (e.g. the admin role selector in the user form) uses the ids to pre-select role items loaded separately, rather than touching the lazy association off a detached entity.

    • setRoles

      @Transactional public PUser setRoles(Long userId, Set<Long> roleIds)
      Replaces the user's assigned roles with exactly the roles identified by roleIds (a full replace: roles not in the set are unassigned, new ones are added). This is the dedicated, elevated-authorization role-assignment operation deliberately excluded from partialUpdateUser(Long, PUser); like the rest of this admin-console service it carries no per-call authorization parameter and is gated by the admin login in front of the UI. user_roles is owned by PUser, so setting the managed collection and saving persists the join rows. A null or empty set clears all roles.
